mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term IC drops below 0.01 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which triggers short-term positive market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat following INDPRO increases, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive federal funds rates creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 and FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below 1.0% signals severe economic deceleration that triggers recession risk pricing, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0%, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm synchronized economic expansion, generating immediate positive market sentiment and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 5-day returns when both conditions are met, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in walk-forward validation.
